title: 控方证人
summary:
After being moved by a woman's plea for loyalty, Dermot leaves a social gathering and walks home through the chilly evening. Upon entering his apartment, he experiences an intense sense of danger. While searching the residence, he discovers an unfamiliar revolver hidden in a drawer, noting that the barrel has been recently fired and that the weapon was not there earlier that evening.

When the doorbell rings, Dermot senses imminent danger and turns off the lights, pretending to be the servant, Milson, to intercept the visitors. Inspector Wylie of Scotland Yard arrives with a police officer, carrying an arrest warrant for Dermot's master, Sir Arlington West, for the murder of an uncle. A second officer discovers the revolver in the apartment and hands it to the Inspector. Constable Cole informs Dermot that the case against West is clear: West and his uncle had been arguing over a will, and a butler named Johnson found the uncle dead of a gunshot wound shortly after.

Realizing the evidence has been planted to frame him, Dermot uses the pretense of making tea to escape through a window. He climbs down an iron bar, cutting his hands in the process. He encounters Jack Trent on the street, who helps him hail a taxi. Trent expresses his belief in Dermot's innocence and notes that a medium at a recent séance had warned against going home. Trent leads Dermot to his own house and into a secret room on the second floor to plan their next move.
